introSection: Stunt Fall is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Parque Warner Madrid in San Martín de la Vega, Community of Madrid, Spain. It is a Giant Inverted Boomerang from Vekoma.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Parque Warner Madrid opened on 6 April 2002; however, Stunt Fall did not open until 8 August. This was the last of four Giant Inverted Boomerang coasters installed by Six Flags (who managed Parque Warner at the time).

This coaster was going to be themed to the 1996 film Twister, but the plans were cancelled.

Stunt Fall was repainted during the 2020 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The supports are orange and the track is blue. The train is silver, orange and blue and has yellow safety bars. It has eight rows and four seats in each row, instead of being in the same line are in the form of "T", i.e. two stations are together and the two ends are further apart allowing for a better view.

fullText:
Stunt Fall is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Parque Warner Madrid in San Martín de la Vega, Community of Madrid, Spain. It is a Giant Inverted Boomerang from Vekoma.

Parque Warner Madrid opened on 6 April 2002; however, Stunt Fall did not open until 8 August. This was the last of four Giant Inverted Boomerang coasters installed by Six Flags (who managed Parque Warner at the time).

This coaster was going to be themed to the 1996 film Twister, but the plans were cancelled.

Stunt Fall was repainted during the 2020 season.

The supports are orange and the track is blue. The train is silver, orange and blue and has yellow safety bars. It has eight rows and four seats in each row, instead of being in the same line are in the form of "T", i.e. two stations are together and the two ends are further apart allowing for a better view.
